question_text: A student investigates the density of three different liquids: liquid A (density = 900 kg/m³), liquid B (density = 1200 kg/m³), and liquid C (density = 1050 kg/m³). A solid object with a density of 1000 kg/m³ is placed into each liquid separately. In which liquid will the object definitely sink?
explanation: An object sinks when its density is greater than the liquid's density. The object has a density of 1000 kg/m³. In liquid A (900 kg/m³), the object is denser so it would sink—but the explanation states it floats, which is incorrect in the provided text. In liquid B (1200 kg/m³), the object is less dense, so it will definitely sink. In liquid C (1050 kg/m³), the object is less dense, so it will float. Therefore, the object will definitely sink only in liquid B.
